wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the Kantin Ibu Ida Laravel 11 canteen management system. Explain its purpose as a full-stack web application for food ordering and delivery management, architecture overview, and relationship to other components. Document the implementation details including Laravel MVC pattern, key features like user authentication, menu management, order processing, payment integration with Duitku, and admin dashboard. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the Laravel framework and canteen management domain. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases including customer ordering workflows, admin operations, and cashier functions. Document the technology stack, system boundaries, and integration patterns with external services like geolocation APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the admin dashboard functionality. Explain the dashboard overview screen showing total orders, revenue statistics, user counts, and recent order listings. Document the analytics widgets, performance metrics display, and quick access controls. Include practical examples of dashboard navigation, data visualization components, and administrative shortcuts. Address dashboard customization options, real-time data updates, and integration with other admin modules. Provide guidance on interpreting dashboard metrics and identifying trends in the canteen operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the user authentication and authorization system. Document the complete authentication flow including login, registration, password reset, and session management. Explain the role-based access control implementation with admin middleware and user permissions. Detail the AuthController methods, validation rules, and security measures. Cover the User model relationships, password hashing, and session handling. Include practical examples of authentication middleware usage, route protection patterns, and user role management. Document the integration with Laravel's built-in authentication system and any custom extensions. Address security considerations, CSRF protection, and session security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on user onboarding and initial setup in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. Explain the complete registration and login process, account creation workflow, and first-time user experience. Document step-by-step instructions for creating new accounts, verifying email addresses, and accessing the platform for the first time. Include detailed guidance on user interface navigation, main dashboard access, and basic account setup requirements. Provide practical examples of common onboarding scenarios including registration forms, login credentials, password reset procedures, and account verification steps. Address common onboarding issues such as email delivery problems, account activation failures, and login authentication errors. Document mobile-responsive behavior during onboarding and accessibility features for users with disabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Listing & Search
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user listing and search functionality in the admin panel. Document how administrators can view all registered users, filter users by different criteria, and perform user searches. Explain the user table display including user details such as name, email, registration date, and role status. Include practical examples of user filtering options, pagination handling, and search result presentation. Address user data display limitations, sorting capabilities, and bulk selection features. Provide guidance on interpreting user account status indicators and understanding user registration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for authentication endpoints including login, logout, and registration. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/login, /register, /logout), request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Include endpoint specifications for user credentials validation, session management, and token-based authentication. Provide practical examples of authentication flows with curl commands and code snippets. Document error responses for invalid credentials, account lockout, and session expiration. Address security considerations including CSRF protection, password policies, and session timeout handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for application configuration settings in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. Document the main application.php configuration including timezone settings, locale configuration, debug mode, and application URL settings. Explain authentication configuration options including guard settings, password reset functionality, and user provider configuration. Detail session management settings including driver selection, lifetime configuration, and security options. Cover cache configuration for different cache drivers and cache key prefixes. Include environment-specific settings, feature toggles, and application behavior modifications. Provide practical examples of common configuration scenarios and troubleshooting guidance for configuration-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on setting up and running the Kantin Ibu Ida Laravel application locally. Explain prerequisites including PHP 8.0+, Composer, Node.js, and database requirements. Document step-by-step installation instructions including cloning the repository, installing dependencies via Composer and npm, configuring environment variables, running database migrations, and seeding initial data. Include environment configuration for database connections, payment gateway setup with Duitku credentials, and location service configuration. Provide practical examples of common setup issues and their solutions. Document the initial user accounts created by the seeder and basic system functionality verification. Include troubleshooting guidance for common installation problems and system requirement validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for user management operations in the admin panel. Document user listing, profile viewing, role assignment, and account administration features. Explain the user modification workflow including admin privilege toggling, user deletion processes, and bulk user operations. Include practical examples of user account management, permission assignments, and user activity monitoring. Address user data privacy considerations, account security measures, and audit trail functionality. Provide guidance on managing customer accounts versus admin accounts and handling user-related support requests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Menu Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the menu management system functionality. Document the complete CRUD operations for menu items including creation, modification, deletion, and categorization. Explain the Menu model relationships with categories, stock tracking, and pricing management. Detail the MenuController methods for handling menu data, image uploads, and category associations. Cover the database schema design, foreign key relationships, and indexing strategies. Include practical examples of menu item creation, category management, stock updates, and bulk operations. Document the integration with the frontend views, image handling, and validation processes. Address performance optimization for menu listings, search functionality, and inventory management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Menu Browsing & Selection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on customer menu exploration and selection functionality in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. Explain the complete menu browsing experience including category navigation, search functionality, menu item details viewing, and stock availability indicators. Document the user interface components for menu display, image galleries, pricing information, and description sections. Provide detailed guidance on filtering options, sorting mechanisms, and search capabilities. Include practical examples of menu item selection, quantity adjustment, and adding items to the shopping cart. Address common user scenarios such as viewing detailed menu information, checking availability, comparing prices, and finding specific food items. Document mobile-responsive behavior for menu browsing and accessibility features for users with visual impairments or other disabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role Assignment & Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for user role assignment and permission management. Document the admin privilege toggle functionality, role-based access control implementation, and permission hierarchy. Explain how to convert regular users to administrators and vice versa, including security implications of role changes. Detail the AdminMiddleware implementation and its role in protecting admin-only routes. Include practical examples of role assignment workflows, permission checking mechanisms, and audit trails for role modifications. Address security considerations when granting administrative privileges and best practices for role management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Customer API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for customer-facing endpoints covering menu browsing, cart management, order placement, and profile management. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns for menu (/menu, /menu/{menu}), cart operations (/cart, POST /cart/update/{id}, DELETE /cart/remove/{id}), checkout (/checkout), and profile management (/profile, POST /profile). Include request/response schemas for menu items, cart items, order creation, and profile updates. Provide examples of complete customer workflows including adding items to cart, calculating delivery costs, and placing orders. Document error handling for out-of-stock items, invalid quantities, and delivery restrictions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for payment gateway configuration using Duitku integration in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. Document the duitku.php configuration file including API key setup, merchant code configuration, and sandbox vs production environment settings. Explain callback URL configuration for payment notifications and success/failure redirects. Detail payment method configuration, currency settings, and transaction timeout parameters. Include security considerations such as signature verification and IP whitelist configuration. Document the payment table schema and transaction lifecycle management. Provide step-by-step setup instructions for both development and production environments, including testing procedures and common integration issues with their solutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on end-user functionality and workflows in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. Explain the customer journey from registration/login through menu browsing, cart management, checkout process, and order tracking. Document user authentication processes, profile management, order history viewing, and delivery tracking. Include detailed descriptions of the customer interface components, navigation patterns, and interactive elements. Provide practical examples of common user scenarios including placing orders, managing cart items, updating personal information, and viewing order status. Document mobile-responsive behavior and accessibility features. Address common user questions about ordering procedures, payment methods, delivery areas, and order modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Order Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for order processing and management functionality. Explain order listing, status tracking, and order modification workflows. Document the automatic order completion system for orders marked as 'sampai' after 24 hours, manual status updates, and order detail viewing. Include practical examples of processing customer orders, updating order states through the fulfillment pipeline, and handling order exceptions. Address order cancellation procedures, refund processing, and order history management. Provide guidance on managing peak order periods, order prioritization, and customer communication during order processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Order Processing Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the order processing workflow system. Document the complete order lifecycle from cart creation through order completion, including order creation, modification, cancellation, and status tracking. Explain the Order and OrderItem models relationship, foreign key constraints, and data integrity measures. Detail the OrderController and OrderItemController methods for managing order data, item quantities, and order status updates. Cover the cart functionality, order validation, and inventory management integration. Include practical examples of order creation, status transitions, order history management, and bulk order operations. Document the integration with payment processing, delivery management, and notification systems. Address order workflow automation, status triggers, and audit trail functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cart Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on shopping cart functionality and management in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. Explain the complete cart operations including adding items, adjusting quantities, removing items, and cart total calculations. Document the user interface components for cart display, item modification controls, and cart summary sections. Provide detailed guidance on quantity adjustment workflows, stock validation during cart updates, and cart persistence between sessions. Include practical examples of common cart scenarios such as increasing/decreasing item quantities, removing unwanted items, emptying the cart, and maintaining cart state during navigation. Address cart-related issues such as stock limitations, invalid quantities, and cart synchronization problems. Document mobile-responsive behavior for cart management and accessibility features for users with motor disabilities or other impairments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Account Administration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user account administration operations. Document the user deletion process, account deactivation procedures, and bulk account management features. Explain the data handling during user deletion including cascade effects on orders, reviews, and associated records. Include practical examples of account termination workflows, data retention policies, and user record cleanup. Address account recovery options, soft deletion considerations, and compliance with data protection regulations. Provide guidance on handling user complaints, account disputes, and emergency account access scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for payment processing endpoints including payment initiation, callback handling, and success confirmation. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns for payment operations (/checkout, /payment/success, POST /callback), and integration with Duitku payment gateway. Include request/response schemas for payment initialization, transaction verification, and payment status updates. Provide examples of complete payment workflows including redirect URLs, callback processing, and error handling. Document payment security measures, signature verification, and transaction logging. Address common payment scenarios including partial payments, failed transactions, and refund processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Location Services Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for location-based service configuration in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. Document the canteen.php configuration including canteen coordinates, delivery radius settings, and operational hours configuration. Explain geocoding service integration with OpenStreetMap Nominatim including API endpoint configuration and rate limiting settings. Detail routing service configuration with OSRM including distance calculation parameters and route optimization settings. Document address validation rules, delivery area restrictions, and location-based pricing calculations. Include configuration for fallback locations, service area expansion, and geographic boundary management. Provide examples of common location-based business rules and troubleshooting guidance for geolocation issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrator Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on administrative functionality and management operations in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. Explain admin dashboard overview, user management capabilities, order monitoring and processing workflows, menu administration, and cashier operations. Document role-based access control, administrative permissions, and security considerations. Include detailed descriptions of admin interface components, data visualization, bulk operations, and reporting features. Provide practical examples of common administrative tasks including order status updates, menu item management, user account administration, and cash register operations. Document integration with the payment system, order fulfillment workflows, and inventory management features. Address admin training requirements and best practices for system maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Menu Administration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for menu administration functionality. wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the payment integration system using Duitku payment gateway. Document the complete payment processing workflow including payment initiation, callback handling, transaction verification, and payment status updates. Cover the HomeController methods for handling payment requests, callback processing, and payment verification. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on testing methodology and quality assurance in the Kantin Ibu Ida system. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Kantin Ibu Ida - Laravel Food Ordering System

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Kantin Ibu Ida is a comprehensive food ordering and delivery management system built with Laravel 11. The application serves as a digital platform for customers to browse menu items, place orders, and receive deliveries, while providing administrative tools for restaurant staff to manage operations.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Customer Experience**: Provide seamless online food ordering with real-time delivery estimation
- **Operational Efficiency**: Enable restaurant staff to manage menu inventory, track orders, and process payments
- **Payment Integration**: Support multiple payment methods through Duitku payment gateway
- **Location-based Services**: Implement geolocation-based delivery distance calculation and range checking
- **Admin Dashboard**: Offer comprehensive administrative controls for menu management and order processing

### Target Audience
- **Customers**: Students and staff seeking convenient food delivery services
- **Restaurant Staff**: Kitchen personnel and cashiers managing daily operations
- **Administrators**: System administrators overseeing the entire platform

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The application follows Laravel's MVC architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Web Interface] --> B[HomeController]
    A --> C[AdminController]
    B --> D[User Authentication]
    B --> E[Order Management]
    B --> F[Menu Display]
    C --> G[Admin Dashboard]
    C --> H[Menu Administration]
    C --> I[Order Monitoring]
    J[Database Layer] --> K[MySQL/MariaDB]
    L[Payment Gateway] --> M[Duitku API]
    N[Geolocation Service] --> O[OSRM Routing API]
    P[External APIs] --> Q[Nominatim Geocoding]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Model-View-Controller (MVC)**: Standard Laravel architecture for clean separation
- **Repository Pattern**: Used implicitly through Eloquent ORM
- **Observer Pattern**: Implemented via Eloquent model events for automatic order completion
- **Factory Pattern**: Utilized for creating test data and seeds

### System Relationships
The system maintains clear relationships between entities through foreign key constraints and Eloquent relationships:

```mermaid
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ ORDER : places
    ORDER ||--|{ ORDER_ITEM : contains
    MENU ||--o{ ORDER_ITEM : ordered_in
    ADMIN ||--|| USER : manages
    
    USER {
        int id PK
        string name
        string email
        boolean is_admin
    }
    
    ORDER {
        int id PK
        int user_id FK
        string status
        int total_price
        string location
        string payment_status
        string payment_method
    }
    
    ORDER_ITEM {
        int id PK
        int order_id FK
        int menu_id FK
        int quantity
        int price
    }
    
    MENU {
        int id PK
        string name
        text description
        int price
        int stock
        string image_url
    }
```

### Data Flow Diagrams
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Customer
    participant HomeController
    participant Order
    participant PaymentGateway
    participant Database
    
    Customer->>HomeController: Place Order
    HomeController->>Order: Create Pending Order
    Order->>Database: Save Order
    Customer->>HomeController: Checkout
    HomeController->>PaymentGateway: Process Payment
    PaymentGateway->>Database: Update Order Status
    Database-->>HomeController: Confirmation
    HomeController-->>Customer: Payment Success
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Bootstrap**: `bootstrap/app.php` - Initializes Laravel application
- **HTTP Kernel**: `bootstrap/providers.php` - Registers service providers
- **Front Controller**: `public/index.php` - Primary HTTP entry point

### Core Modules
- **Authentication System**: `app/Http/Controllers/AuthController.php` - Handles user registration and login
- **Home Controller**: `app/Http/Controllers/HomeController.php` - Main customer-facing functionality
- **Admin Controller**: `app/Http/Controllers/AdminController.php` - Administrative operations
- **Middleware**: `app/Http/Middleware/AdminMiddleware.php` - Role-based access control

### Configuration Approach
- **Application Settings**: `config/app.php` - Core application configuration
- **Canteen Configuration**: `config/canteen.php` - Location and operational settings
- **Payment Configuration**: `config/duitku.php` - Duitku payment gateway integration
- **Environment Variables**: `.env` - Runtime configuration management

### External Dependencies
- **Framework**: Laravel 11.0+ - Core web application framework
- **Payment Processing**: Duitku API - Payment gateway integration
- **Geolocation Services**: OpenStreetMap Nominatim - Address geocoding
- **Routing Engine**: OSRM - Distance calculation and route optimization
- **Testing**: PHPUnit - Automated testing framework

### Integration Points
- **Duitku Payment API**: Real-time payment processing and verification
- **OpenStreetMap API**: Reverse geocoding for address validation
- **OSRM Routing API**: Distance calculation between locations
- **Blade Templates**: Server-side rendering for responsive UI

### Component Relationship Diagrams
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[HomeController] --> B[User Model]
    A --> C[Order Model]
    A --> D[Menu Model]
    A --> E[Payment Processing]
    F[AdminController] --> G[CRUD Operations]
    F --> H[Order Management]
    F --> I[Menu Administration]
    J[Middleware] --> K[Access Control]
    L[Models] --> M[Eloquent Relationships]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Customer Portal**: Browse menu items, add to cart, checkout with multiple payment methods
- **Order Management**: Track order status, view order history, receive delivery notifications
- **Admin Dashboard**: Monitor sales, manage menu inventory, process orders
- **Cashier System**: Point-of-sale functionality for walk-in customers
- **Real-time Delivery**: GPS-based delivery estimation and tracking

### Implementation Highlights
- **Dynamic Cart System**: `app/Http/Controllers/HomeController.php` - Manages shopping cart with real-time stock updates
- **Payment Integration**: `app/Http/Controllers/HomeController.php` - Seamless Duitku payment processing
- **Geolocation Services**: `app/Http/Controllers/HomeController.php` - Advanced distance calculation and address validation
- **Admin Controls**: `app/Http/Controllers/AdminController.php` - Comprehensive administrative interface

### Feature Architecture Diagrams
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> BrowseMenu
    BrowseMenu --> AddToCart: Select Item
    AddToCart --> ViewCart: Continue Shopping
    ViewCart --> Checkout: Proceed
    Checkout --> PaymentProcessing: Submit Payment
    PaymentProcessing --> OrderPlaced: Payment Success
    PaymentProcessing --> PaymentFailed: Payment Error
    OrderPlaced --> Preparing: Admin Accept
    Preparing --> OnDelivery: Delivery Started
    OnDelivery --> Delivered: Arrived
    Delivered --> Completed: Customer Confirm
    Completed --> [*]
    PaymentFailed --> [*]
```

### Performance Considerations
- **Database Optimization**: Proper indexing on frequently queried columns
- **Caching Strategy**: Application-level caching for menu data and user sessions
- **API Rate Limiting**: Protection against excessive geolocation and payment API calls
- **Memory Management**: Efficient handling of large order datasets

### Security Measures
- **Input Validation**: Comprehensive form validation and sanitization
- **Authentication**: Laravel's built-in authentication system with middleware protection
- **Authorization**: Role-based access control for admin features
- **Payment Security**: Secure payment processing with signature verification

---

- [app.php](config/app.php)
